patches.rationale: The chapter DOI returns 404. Replace with verified Springer book URL. The book itself (Resonances in the Earth-Ionosphere Cavity) is a real and citable reference.

patches.find: The Schumann fundamental resonance at 7.83 Hz was published by Schumann in 1952 and has been continuously measured since the 1960s (HeartMath GCI; Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002).
patches.replace: The Schumann fundamental resonance at 7.83 Hz was theoretically predicted by Schumann (1952) and first measured by König & Schumann (1954); it has been continuously monitored since the 1960s (Sentman 1987; Nickolaenko & Hayakawa 2002).
patches.rationale: Fixes two issues: (1) HeartMath 404 link replaced with peer-reviewed Sentman 1987. (2) 'published by Schumann in 1952' was historically wrong — Schumann predicted it theoretically; König & Schumann measured it in 1954. This also addresses ISS-1269.

08-live-power.txt now shows a Domain: P-wave Shadow Zone panel with Predicted/Observed/Tolerance/Status fields. Tag was previously 'none' when no dome monitoring was found; it now exists but with Tolerance: N/A and a static depth-mismatched Observed string — consistent with 'hardcoded' not 'live_fetch'.

patches.replace: (corresponding to 11,600–15,600 km surface distance, using sphere math — which is the point)
patches.rationale: The parenthetical borrows spherical geometry to criticize flat-disc adoption without flagging it. Adding 'using sphere math — which is the point' preempts the confused-reader objection and makes the rhetorical move explicit.

patches.find: This pattern (register prediction → confirm with already-public data within days) recurs across the batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date. It was part of a batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date, suggesting systematic data mining from already-published sources rather than prospective prediction.
patches.replace: This pattern (register prediction → confirm with already-public data within days) recurs across the batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039), all registered on 2026-03-06, suggesting systematic data mining from already-published sources rather than prospective prediction.
patches.rationale: Two consecutive sentences ended with the identical phrase 'batch of five predictions (WIN-035 through WIN-039) all registered on the same date.' Collapsed into one cleaner sentence with the specific date added for precision.

patches.find: ~30 nT over 12–14 months, no live recomputation
patches.replace: ~30 nT over the dome's 14-month reporting window, no live recomputation
patches.rationale: '12-14 months' could be read as us conceding a fuzzy window. Rephrasing to 'dome's 14-month reporting window' attributes the mismatch to the dome's own framing rather than appearing to hedge ourselves.
